brand.name: Paradigm Venture Group
brand.vertical: B2B/professional — enterprise tech consulting with healthcare specialization

offer.what: Enterprise interoperability consulting + AI-powered workflow automation platform (Friday_OS / Krista AI) + custom low-code app building. Core solutions: Enterprise System Interoperability, AI Enterprise Ecosystem, AI-Powered Sales Ecosystem, Conversational AI phone agents, Data Cleaning, Contract Management, Revenue Cycle Management, Rebate & Compliance Management, Advisory Services.
offer.coreOutcome: Eliminate data silos and create a single source of truth across all enterprise systems — automating clinical documentation, billing cycles, supply chain workflows, and sales motions so senior staff stop doing administrative work and start driving revenue.
offer.pricePoint: Not publicly listed. Enterprise B2B project/retainer pricing. 4–6 week deployment timeline cited for AI Enterprise Ecosystem. Likely $25K–$250K+ per engagement depending on scope.
offer.commitment: Project-based engagements with potential for ongoing retainer/managed service. No self-serve; all engagements initiated via contact/discovery call.
leadMagnets: GPT-5 Readiness Audit (free audit — promoted via homepage banner)

audience.icp: Healthcare operations leaders (COO, CIO, VP Supply Chain, Revenue Cycle Directors) at hospitals, IDNs, and health systems who are drowning in disconnected legacy platforms and manual billing/documentation workflows.
painPoints: Disconnected legacy systems creating data silos across ERP, CRM, EHR, and billing
Manual clinical documentation and billing cycles causing revenue leakage
Senior staff burdened with administrative complexity instead of strategic work
No single source of truth — decisions made on stale or fragmented data
Supply chain and contract management inefficiencies driving up costs
Inability to leverage AI because data isn't unified or accessible
segments: Healthcare providers (hospitals, IDNs) — clinical documentation, billing, revenue cycle, rebate & compliance
Enterprise operations teams — supply chain, contract management, ERP/CRM integration
Sales-led businesses — AI sales ecosystem, conversational AI phone agents, CRM automation

salesCycle.trial: Free GPT-5 Readiness Audit as top-of-funnel entry point. No self-serve trial; all entry via 'Contact Us' or 'Get Started' leading to a discovery call.
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Booked discovery call → scoped proposal → signed engagement
salesCycle.topObjection: Integration risk with existing systems (will this break what we already have?) and ROI justification for enterprise-level spend

ahaMoment: The first automated workflow goes live — data flows in real-time between two previously siloed systems (e.g., EHR feeds billing, or CRM syncs ERP) with zero manual intervention. For healthcare clients: first clean billing cycle or first AI-generated clinical report.

retention.recurring: Expansion from initial engagement into additional modules, integrations, or AI capabilities. Potential ongoing retainer for managed interoperability services.
retention.expansion: Add more solutions from their stack (e.g., client starts with Data Cleaning, then adds Revenue Cycle, then AI Enterprise Ecosystem). More departments, more integrations, more AI agents.
retention.churnRisk: Project-mentality clients who treat engagement as one-and-done. No named managed service or SLA model visible on site — risk of 'we're done, thanks' after initial deployment.

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding — no automated onboarding sequence evident for new inquiry/contact
Lead-Magnet Nurture — GPT-5 Readiness Audit has no visible follow-up nurture sequence
Trial/Consult Conversion — no nurture between contact form submission and first discovery call
Booking & Reminders — no automated consultation reminder/confirmation flow visible
Value Newsletter — no thought leadership email program evident
Renewal / Expansion — no triggered expansion or upsell campaign after project completion
Win-Back / Re-Engagement — no re-engagement flow for cold leads or past clients
toneVoice: Professional, forward-thinking, tech-confident. Uses bold enterprise language ('JARVIS-like command layer,' 'semantic interoperability,' 'Single Source of Truth') alongside outcome-focused business copy. Authoritative without being stuffy. Direct, results-first, ambitious.
compliance: Healthcare-adjacent: likely HIPAA considerations given clinical documentation and billing data handling. Standard CAN-SPAM required. Possibly GDPR if serving global enterprise clients. Avoid specific medical/clinical outcome claims in email.
